description_long: This object is a single-piece metal tool resembling a flat wrench or spanner, forged from a solid piece of steel with a rough, unfinished surface. The tool features a straight, flat handle with a circular hole at one end, likely for hanging or storage. At the opposite end, there are two open, angled jaws, each with a flat gripping surface, but lacking the typical pivot mechanism found in standard pliers. The jaws are offset and appear to be designed for gripping or turning nuts and bolts rather than cutting or pinching. The entire tool has a utilitarian, industrial appearance, with visible texture and wear consistent with heavy use. No visible branding or insulation is present, and the tool is entirely metallic.
description: A flat, rough-textured metal tool with a straight handle and two open, angled jaws, resembling a spanner or wrench. The tool is solid steel with a utilitarian, industrial look.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the tool, highlighting the straight handle and the two open, angled jaws at the end.
description_view_1: End-on view focusing on the jaws, showing their offset, open design and the thickness of the metal.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's length, the circular hole at the end, and the open jaws.
description_view_3: View from the handle end, emphasizing the circular hole and the flat, wide body of the tool.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, flat metal handle with a circular hole at the end; no insulation or ergonomic features.
attribute_jaw_design: Open, flat jaws with angled tips; no serration, wire cutter, or notches; jaws are offset and fixed.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: No pivot mechanism; jaws are fixed as part of a single-piece tool.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth, rough-textured metal with no distinct grip pattern or added material.
